events.title: Introduction to Modern Mine Reclamation Strategies
events.description: A free, 90-minute webinar covering regulatory expectations and practical strategies for reclaiming active and legacy mine sites in the western United States.
events.location_details: Live Zoom webinar (link provided upon registration)
events.agenda: Overview of mine reclamation regulations, common pitfalls in reclamation plans, and case studies from recent closure projects.

events.title: Advanced Mine Reclamation Planning Workshop
events.description: A half-day hybrid workshop focused on complex mine closure designs, geomorphic landform reconstruction, and long-term monitoring plans.
events.location_details: In person at Denver Regional Office and via Microsoft Teams
events.agenda: Interactive design charrettes, breakout sessions on reclamation soil cover design, and regulatory coordination best practices.

events.title: Groundwater Monitoring Best Practices for Industrial Sites
events.description: An in-depth webinar on optimizing groundwater monitoring networks, sampling frequency, and data quality for industrial and brownfield sites.
events.agenda: Monitoring network design, low-flow sampling techniques, and trend evaluation using simple statistics.

events.title: Soil Contamination Risk Screening for Redevelopment Projects
events.description: A technical session on screening-level evaluation of soil contamination to support property transactions and redevelopment.
events.agenda: Risk screening frameworks, comparison to regulatory screening levels, and communication with stakeholders.

events.title: UST Compliance Clinic for Fueling Facility Owners
events.description: A practical session for gas station and fleet managers on preventing leaks, managing inspections, and responding to suspected releases.
events.agenda: Inspection readiness, recordkeeping, leak detection technologies, and communication with regulators.

events.title: Mining Water Management Summit
events.description: A two-day summit covering groundwater control, pit lake management, and discharge permitting at metal and coal mines.
events.location_details: Salt Lake City Conference Center, Salt Lake City, UT
events.agenda: Plenary talks, technical breakout sessions, and a regulatory panel on evolving water quality standards.

events.title: Phase I ESA Essentials for Commercial Lenders
events.description: An overview of ASTM E1527-21 requirements and how Phase I ESAs support sound lending decisions.
events.agenda: Key elements of a Phase I ESA, REC identification, and common lender questions.

events.title: In-Situ Remediation Case Studies: Chlorinated Solvents and Petroleum
events.description: Detailed case studies of in-situ chemical oxidation and enhanced bioremediation at industrial sites.
events.agenda: Site characterization, technology selection, implementation challenges, and performance monitoring.

events.title: Mine Closure & Reclamation Roundtable
events.description: A panel discussion with regulators and practitioners on lessons learned from recent mine closure projects.
events.agenda: Stakeholder engagement, long-term water treatment, and financial assurance considerations.

events.title: Groundwater Data Visualization Clinic
events.description: A practical clinic on turning groundwater monitoring results into clear maps, trend charts, and dashboards.
events.agenda: Data structure, visualization tools, and communicating uncertainty to stakeholders.

offices.name: Salt Lake City Mountain West Office

resources.title: Field Guide to Soil Contamination Assessment at Industrial Sites
resources.summary: Step-by-step guidance for planning and executing soil sampling programs at active and legacy industrial facilities.
resources.author: Megan Liu, P.G.
resources.body_html:
Field Guide to Soil Contamination Assessment

This guide provides practical recommendations on developing a conceptual site model, selecting sampling locations, and interpreting results against risk-based screening levels.

resources.title: Managing PFAS in Impacted Soils: Sampling and Treatment Considerations
resources.summary: Technical considerations for sampling, analyzing, and managing soils impacted by per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS).
resources.author: Dr. Alan Rios
resources.body_html:
Managing PFAS in Impacted Soils

PFAS present unique challenges in soil investigation and remediation due to their persistence and mobility. This whitepaper reviews current best practices and evolving regulatory frameworks.

resources.title: Vadose Zone Sampling Techniques for Soil Assessment
resources.summary: An overview of direct-push, hollow-stem auger, and sonic drilling methods for collecting high-quality vadose zone soil samples.
resources.author: Kara Jensen, M.S.
resources.body_html:
Vadose Zone Sampling Techniques

Choosing the right drilling and sampling method is critical to characterizing soil contamination in the vadose zone. This article reviews pros and cons of common techniques.

resources.title: Mine Reclamation Basics: From Plan to Performance Monitoring
resources.summary: A practical introduction to mine reclamation planning, construction, and post-closure monitoring requirements.
resources.author: Laura Kim, P.G.
resources.body_html:
Mine Reclamation Basics

Effective mine reclamation requires integrating geomorphology, hydrology, and regulatory requirements into a cohesive closure plan.

resources.title: Groundwater Monitoring Program Setup Checklist
resources.summary: A practical checklist for designing and implementing a defensible groundwater monitoring program at contaminated sites.
resources.author: Kevin Patel, Ph.D.
resources.body_html:
Groundwater Monitoring Program Setup Checklist

This checklist walks you through defining objectives, selecting monitoring locations, and establishing QA/QC procedures.

resources.title: Phase I ESA Guide for Commercial Property Buyers
resources.summary: Key considerations for buyers commissioning Phase I Environmental Site Assessments for commercial properties.
resources.author: Derek Young, P.G.
resources.body_html:
Phase I ESA Guide for Commercial Property Buyers

Understanding the limitations and strengths of Phase I ESAs can help you better manage environmental risk in transactions.

resources.title: Guide to Reporting and Investigating UST Releases
resources.summary: A stepwise guide for owners and consultants on reporting suspected underground storage tank releases and planning follow-up investigations.
resources.author: Robin Martinez, C.P.E.
resources.body_html:
Guide to Reporting and Investigating UST Releases

This guide outlines when and how to report suspected UST releases, and how to structure an initial investigation work plan.

resources.title: CERCLA and State Superfund Regulatory Update 2025
resources.summary: An annual update on federal and state regulatory changes affecting contaminated site investigation and cleanup.
resources.author: Angela Cho, Esq.
resources.body_html:
CERCLA and State Superfund Regulatory Update 2025

This update summarizes key rule changes, policy guidance, and enforcement trends across major jurisdictions.

resources.title: Soil Vapor Intrusion: Assessment and Mitigation Strategies
resources.summary: Technical guidance on investigating and mitigating soil vapor intrusion risks at commercial and residential properties.
resources.author: Emily Rhodes, P.G.
resources.body_html:
Soil Vapor Intrusion: Assessment and Mitigation Strategies

We discuss screening tools, detailed investigation methods, and common mitigation approaches for soil vapor intrusion.

resources.title: Industrial Site Remediation Case Study Compilation
resources.summary: A collection of brief case studies summarizing soil and groundwater remediation at industrial facilities.
resources.author: Case Study Team
resources.body_html:
Industrial Site Remediation Case Study Compilation

This compilation highlights remediation strategies and outcomes across multiple industrial facilities.

resources.title: Mining Water Quality Monitoring Toolkit
resources.summary: Templates and guidance for designing water quality monitoring programs at active and closed mine sites.
resources.author: Chris Harper, P.G.
resources.body_html:
Mining Water Quality Monitoring Toolkit

This toolkit includes example SAPs, field forms, and reporting templates tailored to mining operations.

resources.title: Using Tracer Tests to Understand Groundwater Flow
resources.summary: An on-demand webinar recording describing how to plan, implement, and interpret groundwater tracer tests.
resources.author: Naomi Feldman, Ph.D.
resources.body_html:
Using Tracer Tests to Understand Groundwater Flow

Tracer tests can provide unique insights into groundwater flow pathways and velocities. This webinar recording explains key concepts and case studies.

services.name: Phase I Environmental Site Assessment (ESA)
services.summary: ASTM-compliant Phase I ESAs for commercial, industrial, and mixed-use properties.
services.description: We perform Phase I Environmental Site Assessments (ESAs) in accordance with ASTM E1527-21 and the EPA All Appropriate Inquiries rule. Our team focuses on delivering clear, decision-ready findings for lenders, buyers, and property owners.
services.scope: Records review, site reconnaissance, interviews, historical research, regulatory database review, and preparation of a concise report identifying recognized environmental conditions (RECs) and data gaps.
services.typical_timeline_text: Most Phase I ESAs for single properties are completed within 2–3 weeks of notice to proceed, with expedited options available for time-sensitive transactions.
services.regulatory_context: Completed in accordance with ASTM E1527-21 and 40 CFR Part 312 (All Appropriate Inquiries), with state-specific considerations addressed where applicable.

services.name: Groundwater Monitoring & Sampling Programs
services.summary: Design and implementation of groundwater monitoring programs for industrial, municipal, and mining clients.
services.description: We design right-sized groundwater monitoring networks to meet regulatory requirements and project objectives. Our services span well installation oversight, routine sampling, data management, and statistical trend evaluation.
services.scope: Monitoring plan development, well installation oversight, low-flow sampling, purge and sampling documentation, data validation, and regulatory-ready reporting.
services.typical_timeline_text: Initial monitoring network design and startup typically occur over 1–2 months, with ongoing sampling events scheduled quarterly, semi-annually, or annually depending on regulatory drivers.
services.regulatory_context: Programs comply with state and federal requirements, including RCRA, CERCLA, UIC, and state groundwater protection programs, with site-specific monitoring and reporting plans.

services.name: Underground Storage Tank Leak Investigation & Remediation
services.summary: Rapid response investigation and cleanup services for leaking underground storage tanks at gas stations and industrial facilities.
services.description: Our UST leak investigation team helps owners and operators respond quickly to suspected or confirmed releases. We coordinate with regulators, characterize soil and groundwater impacts, and design practical remediation strategies that minimize business disruption.
services.scope: Release reporting support, receptor surveys, soil and groundwater investigation, free product recovery, remediation system design, and long-term monitoring and closure documentation.
services.typical_timeline_text: Initial release confirmation and interim response actions are typically implemented within days to weeks, with full site characterization and remedial planning completed over several months depending on site complexity.
services.regulatory_context: Services align with state UST regulations, EPA UST program guidance, and risk-based corrective action (RBCA) frameworks, including coordination with state and local implementing agencies.

services.name: Environmental Site Assessments (Phase I & II)
services.summary: Integrated Phase I and Phase II ESA services to support property transactions and redevelopment.
services.description: We provide turnkey environmental site assessment services, including Phase I ESAs and follow-on Phase II investigations when RECs are identified. Our approach streamlines the transition from due diligence to intrusive investigation.
services.scope: Phase I ESA, Phase II ESA work plan development, soil and groundwater sampling, data evaluation, and risk communication to stakeholders.
services.typical_timeline_text: Combined Phase I/II programs commonly span 1–4 months, depending on the need for regulatory coordination and access to the property.
services.regulatory_context: Work is performed in alignment with ASTM E1527-21, ASTM E1903, and relevant state guidance for site investigation.

services.name: Soil & Groundwater Remediation Design
services.summary: Design and implementation support for in-situ and ex-situ remediation technologies.
services.description: Our remediation specialists develop and optimize remedial strategies for chlorinated solvents, petroleum hydrocarbons, metals, and emerging contaminants. We emphasize practical, measurable solutions tailored to site conditions and regulatory expectations.
services.scope: Technology screening, pilot testing, full-scale design, contractor procurement support, construction oversight, and performance monitoring.
services.typical_timeline_text: From feasibility evaluation through system startup, remediation design projects typically span 6–18 months, with long-term monitoring continuing as needed.
services.regulatory_context: Remedial actions are developed in coordination with state and federal programs under CERCLA, RCRA, UST corrective action, and voluntary cleanup programs.

services.name: Mine Reclamation & Closure Planning
services.summary: Strategic planning and design for mine closure, reclamation, and post-closure monitoring.
services.description: We support mining clients through all stages of mine closure, from early conceptual planning to detailed reclamation design and performance monitoring. Our team integrates geomorphology, hydrology, geochemistry, and regulatory expertise.
services.scope: Closure visioning, reclamation alternatives analysis, grading and cover system design, water management, and long-term monitoring plan development.
services.typical_timeline_text: Mine reclamation planning efforts often span multiple years, but specific planning and design packages can typically be completed within 6–12 months.
services.regulatory_context: Projects are aligned with state mining and reclamation statutes, SMCRA (where applicable), and agency-specific closure and financial assurance requirements.

services.name: Storage Tank Compliance & Management
services.summary: Compliance strategies and inspection support for underground and aboveground storage tanks.
services.description: We help owners and operators maintain compliance across their storage tank portfolios, including periodic inspections, integrity testing coordination, and recordkeeping system setup.
services.scope: Tank registration support, compliance audits, SPCC plan development, inspection scheduling, and staff training.
services.typical_timeline_text: Portfolio-wide compliance reviews are typically completed within 1–3 months, with ongoing support as needed for inspections and renewals.
services.regulatory_context: Services address EPA and state UST/AST regulations, including SPCC and spill response planning requirements.

services.name: Environmental Compliance & Strategy Consulting
services.summary: Strategic advisory services on environmental compliance, permitting, and risk management.
services.description: Our senior consultants provide portfolio-level environmental strategy, helping organizations align compliance obligations with business objectives and risk appetite.
services.scope: Regulatory compliance reviews, permitting strategies, ESG-related risk assessments, and internal program development.
services.typical_timeline_text: Consulting engagements range from short-focus assessments completed in a few weeks to multi-year strategic partnerships.
services.regulatory_context: Advisory services span air, water, waste, and remediation regulations at federal, state, and local levels.

case_studies.title: Chlorinated Solvent Remediation at Electronics Manufacturing Facility
case_studies.summary: Remediation of chlorinated solvent impacts in soil and groundwater at an active electronics manufacturing plant using in-situ chemical reduction.
case_studies.project_background: Historical vapor degreasing operations at the facility resulted in chlorinated solvent releases to shallow groundwater.
case_studies.site_conditions: Urban industrial setting with shallow groundwater at 18 feet bgs, low-permeability silty clays overlying interbedded sandy lenses.
case_studies.approach: Completed high-resolution site characterization to refine the conceptual site model, followed by targeted in-situ chemical reduction injections in source and plume areas.
case_studies.timeline_summary: Investigation, design, and implementation were completed over approximately 24 months, with performance monitoring continuing for two years post-injection.
case_studies.results: Site met risk-based groundwater cleanup goals within three years, and the regulatory agency issued a no-further-action letter.

case_studies.title: Multi-Phase Extraction at Former Rail Yard Maintenance Facility
case_studies.summary: Design and operation of a multi-phase extraction system to address petroleum hydrocarbon impacts at a former rail yard.
case_studies.project_background: A legacy rail yard with historical fueling and maintenance operations required remediation to support redevelopment as a logistics park.
case_studies.site_conditions: Fill materials over native sands and gravels, with residual light non-aqueous phase liquid (LNAPL) present on the water table.
case_studies.approach: Installed a network of extraction wells and designed a multi-phase extraction system to remove LNAPL and contaminated vapors, coupled with aggressive groundwater pumping.
case_studies.timeline_summary: System was designed and installed within 10 months, with two years of active operation followed by confirmatory monitoring.
case_studies.results: LNAPL was removed to below regulatory action thresholds, and the site was approved for industrial reuse with institutional controls.

case_studies.title: Petroleum-Impacted Soil Remediation at Truck Maintenance Depot
case_studies.summary: Excavation and off-site disposal of petroleum-impacted soils at an active truck maintenance facility to address historical releases.
case_studies.project_background: A buyer-commissioned Phase I ESA identified staining and historical spills in the yard, leading to a focused soil investigation.
case_studies.site_conditions: Gravel-surfaced yard with localized areas of stained soil and shallow utilities.
case_studies.approach: Completed targeted soil borings and test pits, then oversaw excavation and disposal of impacted soils, with real-time field screening and confirmation sampling.
case_studies.timeline_summary: From initial assessment to completion of excavation, the project was completed in approximately five months.
case_studies.results: Confirmation samples met industrial land-use soil screening levels and the transaction proceeded without escrow for environmental conditions.

case_studies.title: In-Situ Stabilization of Metal-Impacted Soils at Steel Fabrication Plant
case_studies.summary: Stabilization of lead- and zinc-impacted soils at an active steel fabrication facility to reduce leachability and achieve closure.
case_studies.project_background: Historical sandblasting and slag handling operations had resulted in metals-impacted soils exceeding industrial soil criteria.
case_studies.site_conditions: Large industrial yard with compacted fill and shallow groundwater deeper than 40 feet bgs.
case_studies.approach: Performed bench-scale testing to identify effective reagents, then implemented in-situ mixing and stabilization with rigorous QA/QC testing.
case_studies.timeline_summary: Treatability testing and design required six months, with full-scale implementation completed over the following construction season.
case_studies.results: Post-treatment leachability tests indicated metals concentrations below regulatory leaching thresholds, enabling site closure without excavation.

case_studies.title: Vapor Intrusion Mitigation at Industrial Warehouse Conversion
case_studies.summary: Design and installation of sub-slab depressurization and vapor barriers for an industrial warehouse being converted to mixed-use space.
case_studies.project_background: A former manufacturing building with underlying VOC impacts was redeveloped into a light industrial and office complex.
case_studies.site_conditions: Large slab-on-grade structure with limited access for retrofitting and active construction underway.
case_studies.approach: Conducted pre-design soil vapor assessment, then designed a sub-slab depressurization system and composite vapor barrier integrated with the new slab.
case_studies.timeline_summary: Investigation and design were completed in three months, aligned with the developer’s construction schedule, with system commissioning concurrent with building fit-out.
case_studies.results: Indoor air sampling confirmed that post-mitigation concentrations were below commercial and residential risk-based screening levels.

case_studies.title: Phase I ESA for Multi-Tenant Shopping Center Portfolio
case_studies.summary: Portfolio Phase I ESA assignment covering five shopping centers in two states under a tight transaction timeline.
case_studies.contaminants: Potential petroleum and dry cleaner solvents (no confirmed releases)
case_studies.project_background: A real estate investment firm required consistent environmental due diligence across a small portfolio of neighborhood shopping centers.
case_studies.site_conditions: Urban and suburban retail centers with current and former gas stations and dry cleaners.
case_studies.approach: Standardized scope and reporting formats across all sites, with staggered site visits and parallel records review to meet a compressed closing schedule.
case_studies.timeline_summary: All five reports were delivered within three weeks of notice to proceed.
case_studies.results: No RECs were identified at three properties; two properties were recommended for limited subsurface investigation post-closing.

case_studies.title: Downtown Office Brownfield Redevelopment Support
case_studies.summary: Environmental investigation and risk evaluation to support brownfield redevelopment of a downtown office site.
case_studies.project_background: The client sought to redevelop a former printing facility and warehouse complex into a modern office tower.
case_studies.site_conditions: Tight urban footprint with deep utilities and complex fill materials.
case_studies.approach: Coordinated subsurface investigation with utility locates and traffic control, then completed a risk evaluation consistent with state brownfield guidance.
case_studies.timeline_summary: Investigation and reporting were completed over eight months to align with entitlement milestones.
case_studies.results: Regulators approved a conditional closure with engineering controls that accommodated the planned building design.

case_studies.title: Heap Leach Pad Closure and Reclamation at Gold Mine
case_studies.summary: Design of final cover systems and stormwater controls for closure of a legacy heap leach pad at a gold mine.
case_studies.contaminants: Residual cyanide and metals in spent ore
case_studies.project_background: The mine required closure of a legacy heap leach pad to meet state reclamation and water quality permit obligations.
case_studies.site_conditions: Semi-arid climate with steep, high-elevation pad slopes and limited growth media.
case_studies.approach: Developed and evaluated multiple cover configurations, optimizing for long-term stability, infiltration control, and constructability.
case_studies.timeline_summary: Conceptual through final design were completed over 14 months, with construction phased over two seasons.
case_studies.results: Regulators approved the closure design, and post-construction inspections confirmed stable slopes and adequate vegetation establishment.

case_studies.title: Municipal Landfill Closure and Capping
case_studies.summary: Engineering design and oversight for closure of an unlined municipal landfill, including composite cap and gas management.
case_studies.contaminants: Municipal solid waste, landfill gas, leachate
case_studies.project_background: A mid-sized municipality needed to close a legacy landfill in compliance with updated state solid waste regulations.
case_studies.site_conditions: Gently sloping landfill with existing partial cover and nearby surface water.
case_studies.approach: Developed a composite geomembrane and soil cover system, integrated with landfill gas collection and surface water controls.
case_studies.timeline_summary: Design, permitting, and construction oversight were completed over three years.
case_studies.results: The landfill achieved regulatory closure, with ongoing post-closure monitoring confirming cap performance and gas control.

case_studies.title: Vapor Intrusion Evaluation at Residential Subdivision
case_studies.summary: Investigation of potential vapor intrusion risks at a residential subdivision adjacent to an industrial dry cleaner.
case_studies.contaminants: PCE, TCE in soil gas and indoor air
case_studies.project_background: Residents near an industrial dry cleaner raised concerns about potential vapor intrusion into homes.
case_studies.site_conditions: Mixed-age residential neighborhood underlain by shallow, fractured bedrock.
case_studies.approach: Implemented a phased sampling program and worked with public health officials to communicate findings and risk management options.
case_studies.timeline_summary: Fieldwork and reporting were completed within six months.
case_studies.results: Data supported the conclusion that mitigation was not required at most homes; targeted mitigation was recommended at two properties.

case_studies.title: Groundwater Plume Delineation at Food Processing Plant
case_studies.summary: Comprehensive groundwater investigation to delineate a nitrate and ammonia plume downgradient of a food processing facility.
case_studies.project_background: Historical wastewater management practices had contributed to nitrate impacts in groundwater downgradient of the facility.
case_studies.site_conditions: Alluvial aquifer with moderate hydraulic conductivity, surrounded by mixed agricultural and residential land uses.
case_studies.approach: Installed a multi-level monitoring well network and completed seasonal sampling to inform a groundwater flow and transport model.
case_studies.timeline_summary: Investigation activities were completed within 18 months.
case_studies.results: The plume was fully delineated and a long-term monitoring and mitigation strategy was developed with regulators.

case_studies.title: Tailings Pond Stability and Risk Assessment
case_studies.summary: Geotechnical and hydrologic assessment of a legacy tailings impoundment to evaluate stability and failure risks.
case_studies.project_background: The mine owner sought an independent stability review of a legacy tailings impoundment in light of evolving global tailings standards.
case_studies.site_conditions: Large tailings impoundment with downstream residential and ecological receptors.
case_studies.approach: Combined field investigations with numerical modeling and a structured risk evaluation consistent with emerging industry guidance.
case_studies.timeline_summary: The assessment was completed within 12 months, including workshops with internal and external stakeholders.
case_studies.results: Recommended targeted buttressing and improved water management, which the client implemented as part of a broader risk reduction program.

service_packages.name: Basic Groundwater Monitoring Package
service_packages.description: Entry-level monitoring program suitable for small sites with stable plumes, including semi-annual sampling at up to four wells.
included_analyses: Target VOCs or petroleum constituents (up to 10 analytes)

service_packages.name: Standard Groundwater Monitoring Package
service_packages.description: Quarterly groundwater monitoring for up to eight wells, including a concise annual data summary.
included_analyses: Expanded VOC or petroleum suite (up to 25 analytes)
Metals (dissolved and total) at one event per year

service_packages.name: Advanced Groundwater Monitoring Package
service_packages.description: High-resolution monitoring program with up to six sampling events per year and enhanced data analysis for trend evaluation.

service_packages.name: Premium Compliance Monitoring Package
service_packages.description: Full-compliance monitoring for complex sites, with monthly or event-driven sampling and detailed quarterly reports.

service_packages.name: Phase I ESA – Single Commercial Property
service_packages.description: ASTM E1527-21-compliant Phase I ESA for a single commercial property up to 5 acres.
included_analyses: Historical aerial and fire insurance review

service_packages.name: Phase I ESA – 5-Site Portfolio Package
service_packages.description: Bundled Phase I ESA services for up to five properties in the same region with harmonized deliverables.

service_packages.name: Phase I ESA – 10-Business-Day Rush
service_packages.description: Expedited Phase I ESA delivery within 10 business days of access and records receipt.

service_packages.name: UST Leak Rapid Response Package
service_packages.description: Initial response and investigation package for suspected UST releases at fueling facilities.

service_packages.name: Mine Reclamation Conceptual Planning Package
service_packages.description: High-level closure and reclamation concepts, schedule, and cost opinion for active or legacy mines.

service_packages.name: Mine Reclamation Detailed Design Package
service_packages.description: Full engineering design of reclamation earthworks, cover systems, and water management features.

service_packages.name: Environmental Compliance & ESG Strategy Retainer
service_packages.description: Annual consulting retainer for ongoing environmental compliance and ESG strategy support.

team_members.bio: Sophia leads complex hydrogeologic investigations and remediation projects across California, with a focus on groundwater-surface water interaction and regulatory strategy.
team_members.qualifications: M.S. Hydrogeology; California Professional Geologist (PG); Certified Hydrogeologist (CHG).

team_members.bio: Carlos specializes in groundwater remediation at complex industrial and port facilities, managing multi-stakeholder projects throughout Southern California.
team_members.qualifications: M.S. Civil and Environmental Engineering; California PG; CHG.

team_members.bio: Megan leads site characterization programs for industrial and commercial clients, with extensive experience in soil and bedrock investigations.
Kevin manages regional groundwater monitoring networks and provides technical oversight for complex flow and transport modeling projects.
Laura leads the firm’s mining reclamation practice, helping clients navigate closure planning, reclamation design, and stakeholder engagement.
Robin focuses on UST compliance, corrective action, and remedial system optimization for fueling facilities and fleet yards.
Emily manages soil and soil vapor investigations at industrial and commercial sites, with a strong focus on data quality and risk communication.
Chris designs water management systems for mines and industrial facilities, including surface water controls and treatment systems.
team_members.qualifications: P.E. (WA, OR); M.S. Water Resources Engineering.

team_members.bio: Angela advises clients on environmental regulatory compliance, enforcement negotiations, and policy developments.
team_members.qualifications: J.D.; admitted to practice in California.

team_members.bio: Naomi specializes in advanced investigative techniques, including tracer tests and isotopic studies, to understand complex groundwater systems.
Taylor manages multi-site portfolios of due diligence and remediation projects, keeping complex schedules and stakeholder needs aligned.
Jordan supports field investigations for soil, groundwater, and mine reclamation projects throughout the Mountain West.
Alex supports soil and groundwater investigations at refining, petrochemical, and logistics facilities along the Gulf Coast.
